message:
feat(server): redesign startup banner, harden console input loop, and lazy-init metrics schema

Startup banner (console.rs): Replace the flat key=value layout with an
ASCII-art logo and structured box-drawing sections ("Server Configuration"
and "Database Statistics"). Each stat gets its own labeled line instead of
being crammed into a single "Stats:" line, improving readability in both
wide and narrow terminals. Path truncation widened from 60 to 70 chars.

Console input worker (lib.rs): The previous input loop silently swallowed
poll_event/read_event errors and had no back-pressure mechanisms. Now:
- Errors are logged (once per transition) and the worker backs off 25ms
  before retrying, avoiding tight error-spin loops.
- An "empty ready streak" counter detects terminals that report ready but
  yield no events (e.g. pseudo-tty edge cases); after 8 such streaks the
  worker sleeps 2ms to avoid burning CPU.
- A "drain cap streak" counter limits sustained 128-event drain cycles;
  after 3 consecutive cap-hits the worker sleeps 1ms before resuming,
  preventing hot terminal streams from starving HTTP/tool threads.
- HTTP runtime explicitly sets enable_parking(true) to guard against
  upstream Tokio default drift.

Tool metrics (tool_metrics.rs): Remove the unconditional ensure_metrics_schema()
call on every read path (load_latest_persisted_metrics, persisted_metric_store_size).
Instead, attempt the query first and only run DDL lazily if the error is
specifically "no such table: tool_metrics_snapshots". This avoids a
redundant write-lock + PRAGMA on every poll cycle, reducing SQLite
contention under high TUI refresh rates. New helper
is_missing_metrics_table_error() with unit tests.

TUI app (tui_app.rs): Cap deferred-action drain to 64 per tick
(MAX_DEFERRED_ACTIONS_PER_TICK). If the cap is hit, a follow-up
HousekeepingTick is scheduled so remaining actions are processed on the
next frame rather than being lost. New test verifies the cap behavior.

TUI poller (tui_poller.rs): Clamp with_interval() override to a 10ms
floor (MIN_OVERRIDE_POLL_INTERVAL) to prevent accidental zero-duration
busy-loops in tests or manual configuration. New test covers the clamp.

System health (system_health.rs): Increase WORKER_SLEEP from 50ms to
500ms. The health-check worker was waking 20x/sec to re-probe endpoints
that change at most every few seconds; 2x/sec is sufficient and reduces
background CPU overhead.

TUI bridge / screens / mail_ui / retention: Formatting-only changes
(trailing whitespace, long-line reformats, suppress unused-Result warning
in test). No logic changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
